\"test-access-key\",\n415\t\t\tAuthSecretKey: \"test-secret-key\",\n416\t\t}\n417\t\n418\t\tmb := newMockBackend()\n419\t\tcache := backend.NewMetadataCache(1000, 300)\n420\t\tfooterCache := backend.NewFooterCache(1000, 300)\n421\t\n422\t\t// Create a KeyManager with the generated MEK\n423\t\tkm, err := keymanager.New(mek, nil, nil)\n424\t\tif err != nil {\n425\t\t\tt.Fatalf(\"failed to create key manager: %v\", err)\n426\t\t}\n427\t\n428\t\treturn cfg, mb, cache, footerCache, km\n429\t}\n430\t\n431\tfunc TestPutObjectGetObject(t *testing.T) {\n432\t\tcfg, mb, cache, footerCache, km status 200, got %d\", w.Code)\n640\t\t}\n641\t\n642\t\t// Parse XML response\n643\t\tvar result struct {\n644\t\t\tContents []struct {\n645\t\t\t\tKey  string `xml:\"Key\"`\n646\t\t\t\tSize int64  `xml:\"Size\"`\n647\t\t\t} `xml:\"Contents\"`\n648\t\t}\n649\t\n650\t\tif err := xml.Unmarshal(w.Body.Bytes(), &result); err != nil {\n651\t\t\tt.Fatalf(\"failed to parse XML: %v\", %s: %d\", obj.Key, obj.Size)\n662\t\t\t}\n663\t\t}\n664\t}\n665\t\n666\tfunc TestEncryptionRoundTrip(t *testing.T) {\n667\t\t// This test verifies that the encryption actually happens\n668\t\t// by checking that the stored data is different from plaintext\n669\t\tcfg, mb, cache, footerCache, mek := testSetup(t)\n670\t\th := handlers.New(cfg, mb, cache, footerCache, mek, nil)\n671\t\n672\t\tplaintext := []byte(\"This is sensitive data that should be encrypted\")\n673\t\n674\t\treq := httptest.NewRequest(http.MethodPut, \"/test-bucket/encrypted\", bytes.NewReader(plaintext))\n675\t\tw := httptest.NewRecorder()\n676\t\th.HandleRoot(w, req)\n677\t\n678\t\t// Check that stored data is encrypted (not plaintext)\n679\t\tmb.mu.Lock()\n680\t\tstoredData := mb.objects[\"test-bucket/encrypted\"]\n681\t\tmb.mu.Unlock()\n682\t\n683\t\tif bytes.Contains(storedData, plaintext) {\n684\t\t\tt.Error(\"plaintext found in stored data - encryption may not be working\")\n685\t\t}\n686\t\n687\t\t// Verify envelope magic is present (ARMR header)\n688\t\tif len(storedData) < 4 || string(storedData[:4]) != \"ARMR\" {\n689\t\t\tt.Error(\"ARMR magic not found at start of stored data\")\n690\t\t}\n691\t}\n692\t\n693\tfunc TestMultipleFilesSameMEK(t *testing.T) {\n694\t\t// Test httptest.NewRecorder()\n720\t\t\th.HandleRoot(w, req)\n721\t\n722\t\t\tif w.Code != http.StatusOK {\n723\t\t\t\tt.Errorf(\"failed to download %s: status %d\", key, w.Code)\n724\t\t\t\tcontinue\n725\t\t\t}\n726\t\n727\t\t\tif !bytes.Equal(w.Body.Bytes(), expectedContent) {\n728\t\t\t\tt.Errorf(\"content mismatch for %s\", key)\n729\t\t\t}\n730\t\t}\n731\t}\n732\t\n733\tfunc TestNonARMORObjectPassthrough(t *testing.T) {\n734\t\t// Test that non-ARMOR objects (without x-amz-meta-armor-version) pass through unchanged\n735\t\tcfg, mb, cache, footerCache, mek := testSetup(t)\n736\t\th := handlers.New(cfg, mb, cache, footerCache, mek, nil)\n737\t\n738\t\t// Store a plain object directly in the mock backend\n739\t\tplainData := []byte(\"Plain unencrypted data\")\n740\t\tmb.Put(context.Background(), \"test-bucket\", \"plain-file\", bytes.NewReader(plainData), int64(len(plainData)), {\n760\t\t// Test that ETag is consistent across uploads of same content\n761\t\tcfg, mb, cache, footerCache, mek := testSetup(t)\n762\t\th := handlers.New(cfg, mb, cache, footerCache, mek, nil)\n763\t\n764\t\tcontent := []byte(\"Same content\")\n765\t\n766\t\t// Upload twice\n767\t\treq1 := httptest.NewRequest(http.MethodPut, \"/test-bucket/etag-test-1\", bytes.NewReader(content))\n768\t\treq1.Header.Set(\"Content-Type\", \"text/plain\")\n769\t\tw1 := httptest.NewRecorder()\n770\t\th.HandleRoot(w1, req1)\n771\t\tetag1 := w1.Header().Get(\"ETag\")\n772\t\n773\t\treq2 := httptest.NewRequest(http.MethodPut, \"/test-bucket/etag-test-2\", bytes.NewReader(content))\n774\t\treq2.Header.Set(\"Content-Type\", \"text/plain\")\n775\t\tw2 := httptest.NewRecorder()\n776\t\th.HandleRoot(w2, req2)\n777\t\tetag2 := w2.Header().Get(\"ETag\")\n778\t\n779\t\tif etag1 != etag2 {\n780\t\t\tt.Errorf(\"ETags for same content differ: %s vs %s\", etag1, etag2)\n781\t\t}\n782\t\n783\t\t// Upload different content\n784\t\tdifferentContent := []byte(\"Different content\")\n785\t\treq3 := httptest.NewRequest(http.MethodPut, \"/test-bucket/etag-test-3\", bytes.NewReader(differentContent))\n786\t\treq3.Header.Set(\"Content-Type\", \"text/plain\")\n787\t\tw3 := httptest.NewRecorder()\n788\t\th.HandleRoot(w3, req3)\n789\t\tetag3 := w3.Header().Get(\"ETag\")\n790\t\n791\t\tif etag1 == etag3 {\n792\t\t\tt.Error(\"ETags for different content are the same\")\n793\t\t}\n794\t}\n795\t\n796\t// TestHMACVerification ensures HMAC verification catches tampering\n797\t// With streaming decryption, the HTTP status is written before all blocks are verified.\n798\t// Tampering is detected during streaming, which will cause the body to be incomplete.\n799\t// The httptest.ResponseRecorder doesn't capture streaming errors, so we verify\n800\t// that the body content is incomplete or corrupted rather than checking status code.\n801\tfunc TestHMACVerification(t *testing.T) {\n802\t\tcfg, mb, cache, footerCache, mek := testSetup(t)\n803\t\th := handlers.New(cfg, mb, cache, footerCache, mek, nil)\n804\t\n805\t\tplaintext := []byte(\"Content to verify\")\n806\t\n807\t\treq := httptest.NewRequest(http.MethodPut, \"/test-bucket/verify-test\", bytes.NewReader(plaintext))\n808\t\treq.Header.Set(\"Content-Type\", \"text/plain\")\n809\t\tw := httptest.NewRecorder()\n810\t\th.HandleRoot(w, req)\n811\t\n812\t\t// Tamper with stored data\n813\t\tmb.mu.Lock()\n814\t\tstored := mb.objects[\"test-bucket/verify-test\"]\n815\t\t// Modify a byte in the encrypted data section (after header)\n816\t\tif len(stored) > 100 {\n817\t\t\tstored[80] ^= 0xFF\n818\t\t}\n819\t\tmb.mu.Unlock()\n820\t\n821\t\t// GET should fail integrity check\n822\t\t// With streaming, the status is written before HMAC verification completes.\n823\t\t// The verification error will cause incomplete/corrupted body content.\n824\t\treq = httptest.NewRequest(http.MethodGet, \"/test-bucket/verify-test\", nil)\n825\t\tw = httptest.NewRecorder()\n826\t\th.HandleRoot(w, req)\n827\t\n828\t\t// Verify the response is not the original plaintext (tampering detected)\n829\t\t// Either the body is empty/incomplete, or it's corrupted\n830\t\tif bytes.Equal(w.Body.Bytes(), plaintext) {\n831\t\t\tt.Error(\"expected corrupted/incomplete data for tampered object, got original plaintext\")\n832\t\t}\n833\t\n834\t\t// For range requests, we still get 500 because HMAC is verified before streaming\n835\t\t// But for full downloads with streaming, we may get 200 with incomplete body\n836\t\t// This is expected behavior - streaming provides lower latency at the cost of\n837\t\t// potentially incomplete responses on integrity failure\n838\t}\n839\t\n840\t// TestRangeSuffixRequest tests suffix range requests (e.g., \"bytes=-100\")\n841\tfunc TestRangeSuffixRequest(t *testing.T) {\n842\t\tcfg, mb, cache, footerCache, mek := testSetup(t)\n843\t\th := handlers.New(cfg, mb, cache, footerCache, mek, nil)\n844\t\n845\t\tplaintext := make([]byte, 10000)\n846\t\tfor i := range plaintext {\n847\t\t\tplaintext[i] = byte(i % 256)\n848\t\t}\n849\t\n850\t\treq := httptest.NewRequest(http.MethodPut, \"/test-bucket/suffix-test\", bytes.NewReader(plaintext))\n851\t\treq.Header.Set(\"Content-Type\", \"application/octet-stream\")\n852\t\tw := httptest.NewRecorder()\n853\t\th.HandleRoot(w, req)\n854\t\n855\t\t// Request last 100 bytes\n856\t\treq = httptest.NewRequest(http.MethodGet, \"/test-bucket/suffix-test\", nil)\n857\t\treq.Header.Set(\"Range\", \"bytes=-100\")\n858\t\tw = httptest.NewRecorder()\n859\t\n860\t\th.HandleRoot(w, req)\n861\t\n862\t\tif w.Code != http.StatusPartialContent {\n863\t\t\tt.Errorf(\"expected status 206, got %d\", w.Code)\n864\t\t}\n865\t\n866\t\texpected := plaintext[len(plaintext)-100:]\n867\t\tif !bytes.Equal(w.Body.Bytes(), expected) {\n868\t\t\tt.Error(\"suffix range content mismatch\")\n869\t\t}\n870\t}\n871\t\n872\t// TestStreamingDecryption verifies that pipelined streaming decryption works correctly\n873\t// for multi-block files. This exercises the io.Pipe based streaming path.\n874\tfunc TestStreamingDecryption(t *testing.T) {\n875\t\tcfg, mb, cache, footerCache, mek := testSetup(t)\n876\t\th := handlers.New(cfg, mb, cache, footerCache, mek, nil)\n877\t\n878\t\t// Create a file spanning multiple blocks (64KB each)\n879\t\t// Using 3 blocks worth of data\n880\t\tblockSize := cfg.BlockSize\n881\t\tplaintext := make([]byte, blockSize*3-1000) // Not aligned to block boundary\n882\t\tfor i := range plaintext {\n883\t\t\tplaintext[i] = byte(i % 256)\n884\t\t}\n885\t\n886\t\t// Upload\n887\t\treq := httptest.NewRequest(http.MethodPut, \"/test-bucket/streaming-test\", bytes.NewReader(plaintext))\n888\t\treq.Header.Set(\"Content-Type\", \"application/octet-stream\")\n889\t\tw := httptest.NewRecorder()\n890\t\th.HandleRoot(w, req)\n891\t\n892\t\tif w.Code != http.StatusOK {\n893\t\t\tt.Fatalf(\"PUT failed: status %d\", w.Code)\n894\t\t}\n895\t\n896\t\t// Download with full streaming (no Range header)\n897\t\treq = httptest.NewRequest(http.MethodGet, \"/test-bucket/streaming-test\", nil)\n898\t\tw = httptest.NewRecorder()\n899\t\th.HandleRoot(w, req)\n900\t\n901\t\tif w.Code != http.StatusOK {\n902\t\t\tt.Errorf(\"GET failed: status %d\", w.Code)\n903\t\t}\n904\t\n905\t\t// Verify streaming header is present\n906\t\tif w.Header().Get(\"X-Armor-Stream\") != \"pipelined\" {\n907\t\t\tt.Error(\"expected X-Armor-Stream: pipelined header\")\n908\t\t}\n909\t\n910\t\t// Verify content matches exactly\n911\t\tif !bytes.Equal(w.Body.Bytes(), plaintext) {\n912\t\t\tt.Errorf(\"streaming content mismatch: got %d bytes, want %d bytes\",\n913\t\t\t\tlen(w.Body.Bytes()), len(plaintext))\n914\t\t}\n915\t\n916\t\t// Verify Accept-Ranges header\n917\t\tif w.Header().Get(\"Accept-Ranges\") != \"bytes\" {\n918\t\t\tt.Error(\"expected Accept-Ranges: bytes header\")\n919\t\t}\n920\t}\n921\t\n922\t// TestStreamingDecryptionVariousSizes tests streaming with different file sizes\n923\tfunc TestStreamingDecryptionVariousSizes(t *testing.T) {\n924\t\tsizes := []int{\n925\t\t\t100,           // Tiny\n926\t\t\t4096,          // One 4KB page\n927\t\t\t65535,         // Just under one block\n928\t\t\t65536,         // Exactly one block\n929\t\t\t65537,         // Just over one block\n930\t\t\t131072,        // Two blocks\n931\t\t\t1000000,       // ~1MB\n932\t\t}\n933\t\n934\t\tfor _, size := range sizes {\n935\t\t\tt.Run(fmt.Sprintf(\"size_%d\", size), func(t *testing.T) {\n936\t\t\t\tcfg, mb, cache, footerCache, mek := testSetup(t)\n937\t\t\t\th := handlers.New(cfg, mb, cache, footerCache, mek, nil)\n938\t\n939\t\t\t\tplaintext := make([]byte, size)\n940\t\t\t\tfor i := range plaintext {\n941\t\t\t\t\tplaintext[i] = byte(i % 256)\n942\t\t\t\t}\n943\t\n944\t\t\t\tkey := fmt.Sprintf(\"streaming-test-%d\", size)\n945\t\t\t\treq := httptest.NewRequest(http.MethodPut, \"/test-bucket/\"+key, bytes.NewReader(plaintext))\n946\t\t\t\treq.Header.Set(\"Content-Type\", \"application/octet-stream\")\n947\t\t\t\tw := httptest.NewRecorder()\n948\t\t\t\th.HandleRoot(w, req)\n949\t\n950\t\t\t\tif w.Code != http.StatusOK {\n951\t\t\t\t\tt.Fatalf(\"PUT failed: status %d\", w.Code)\n952\t\t\t\t}\n953\t\n954\t\t\t\treq = httptest.NewRequest(http.MethodGet, \"/test-bucket/\"+key, nil)\n955\t\t\t\tw = httptest.NewRecorder()\n956\t\t\t\th.HandleRoot(w, req)\n957\t\n958\t\t\t\tif w.Code != http.StatusOK {\n959\t\t\t\t\tt.Errorf(\"GET failed: status %d\", w.Code)\n960\t\t\t\t}\n961\t\n962\t\t\t\tif !bytes.Equal(w.Body.Bytes(), plaintext) {\n963\t\t\t\t\tt.Errorf(\"content mismatch for size %d: got %d bytes, want %d\",\n964\t\t\t\t\t\tsize, len(w.Body.Bytes()), size)\n965\t\t\t\t}\n966\t\t\t})\n967\t\t}\n968\t}\n969\t\n970\t// TestCopyObject tests S3 CopyObject with ARMOR encryption\n971\tfunc TestCopyObject(t *testing.T) {\n972\t\tcfg, mb, cache, footerCache, mek := testSetup(t)\n973\t\th := handlers.New(cfg, mb, cache, footerCache, mek, nil)\n974\t\n975\t\t// Upload source file\n976\t\tsrcContent := []byte(\"Source content to copy\")\n977\t\treq := httptest.NewRequest(http.MethodPut, \"/test-bucket/source-file.txt\", bytes.NewReader(srcContent))\n978\t\treq.Header.Set(\"Content-Type\", \"text/plain\")\n979\t\tw := httptest.NewRecorder()\n980\t\th.HandleRoot(w, req)\n981\t\n982\t\tif w.Code != http.StatusOK {\n983\t\t\tt.Fatalf(\"PUT failed: status %d\", w.Code)\n984\t\t}\n985\t\n986\t\t// Copy the file\n987\t\treq = httptest.NewRequest(http.MethodPut, \"/test-bucket/dest-file.txt\", nil)\n988\t\treq.Header.Set(\"x-amz-copy-source\", \"/test-bucket/source-file.txt\")\n989\t\tw = httptest.NewRecorder()\n990\t\th.HandleRoot(w, req)\n991\t\n992\t\tif w.Code != http.StatusOK {\n993\t\t\tt.Errorf(\"COPY failed: status %d, body: %s\", w.Code, TestCopyObjectRewrapsDEK tests that CopyObject re-wraps the DEK\n1016\tfunc TestCopyObjectRewrapsDEK(t *testing.T) {\n1017\t\tcfg, mb, cache, footerCache, mek := testSetup(t)\n1018\t\th := handlers.New(cfg, mb, cache, footerCache, mek, nil)\n1019\t\n1020\t\t// Upload source file\n1021\t\tsrcContent := []byte(\"Content with key that should be re-wrapped\")\n1022\t\treq := httptest.NewRequest(http.MethodPut, \"/test-bucket/rewrap-source.txt\", bytes.NewReader(srcContent))\n1023\t\treq.Header.Set(\"Content-Type\", \"text/plain\")\n1024\t\tw := httptest.NewRecorder()\n1025\t\th.HandleRoot(w, req)\n1026\t\n1027\t\t// Get the original wrapped DEK\n1028\t\tmb.mu.Lock()\n1029\t\tsrcMeta := mb.meta[\"test-bucket/rewrap-source.txt\"]\n1030\t\toriginalWrappedDEK := srcMeta[\"x-amz-meta-armor-wrapped-dek\"]\n1031\t\tmb.mu.Unlock()\n1032\t\n1033\t\t// Copy the file\n1034\t\treq = httptest.NewRequest(http.MethodPut, \"/test-bucket/rewrap-dest.txt\", nil)\n1035\t\treq.Header.Set(\"x-amz-copy-source\", \"/test-bucket/rewrap-source.txt\")\n1036\t\tw = httptest.NewRecorder()\n1037\t\th.HandleRoot(w, req)\n1038\t\n1039\t\tif w.Code != http.StatusOK {\n1040\t\t\tt.Errorf(\"COPY failed: status %d\", w.Code)\n1041\t\t}\n1042\t\n1043\t\t// Get the destination's wrapped DEK - should be different (re-wrapped)\n1044\t\t// even though it wraps the same DEK\n1045\t\tmb.mu.Lock()\n1046\t\tdstMeta := mb.meta[\"test-bucket/rewrap-dest.txt\"]\n1047\t\tnewWrappedDEK := dstMeta[\"x-amz-meta-armor-wrapped-dek\"]\n1048\t\tmb.mu.Unlock()\n1049\t\n1050\t\t// The wrapped DEK should be the same since we're using the same MEK\n1051\t\t// (re-wrapping with same MEK produces same output due to AES-KWP)\n1052\t\t// But the important thing is that decryption still works\n1053\t\tif newWrappedDEK == \"\" {\n1054\t\t\tt.Error(\"destination missing wrapped DEK\")\n1055\t\t}\n1056\t\n1057\t\t// Verify we can still decrypt\n1058\t\treq = httptest.NewRequest(http.MethodGet, \"/test-bucket/rewrap-dest.txt\", nil)\n1059\t\tw = httptest.NewRecorder()\n1060\t\th.HandleRoot(w, req)\n1061\t\n1062\t\tif !bytes.Equal(w.Body.Bytes(), srcContent) {\n1063\t\t\tt.Error(\"decrypted content does not match original\")\n1064\t\t}\n1065\t\n1066\t\t// Log for visibility\n1067\t\tt.Logf(\"Original wrapped DEK length: %d\", len(originalWrappedDEK))\n1068\t\tt.Logf(\"New wrapped DEK length: %d\", len(newWrappedDEK))\n1069\t}\n1070\t\n1071\t// TestCopyObjectNonARMOR tests copying non-ARMOR objects\n1072\tfunc TestCopyObjectNonARMOR(t *testing.T) {\n1073\t\tcfg, mb, cache, TestDeleteObjects tests S3 DeleteObjects (bulk delete)\n1158\tfunc TestDeleteObjects(t *testing.T) {\n1159\t\tcfg, mb, cache, footerCache, mek := testSetup(t)\n1160\t\th := handlers.New(cfg, mb, len(prefixResult.Contents))\n1700\t\t}\n1701\t\n1702\t\t// Test with delimiter on empty bucket\n1703\t\treq = httptest.NewRequest(http.MethodGet, \"/test-bucket?list-type=2&delimiter=/\", got %d\", w.Code)\n1710\t\t}\n1711\t}\n1712\t\n1713\t// TestConditionalRequests tests If-Match, If-None-Match, If-Modified-Since, If-Unmodified-Since\n1714\tfunc TestConditionalRequests(t *testing.T) {\n1715\t\tcfg, mb, cache, footerCache, mek := testSetup(t)\n1716\t\th := handlers.New(cfg, mb, cache, footerCache, mek, nil)\n1717\t\n1718\t\t// Create and upload an object\n1719\t\tplaintext := []byte(\"Hello, ARMOR! This is a test file for conditional requests.\")\n1720\t\treq := httptest.NewRequest(http.MethodPut, \"/test-bucket/conditional-test\", bytes.NewReader(plaintext))\n1721\t\treq.Header.Set(\"Content-Type\", \"text/plain\")\n1722\t\tw := httptest.NewRecorder()\n1723\t\th.HandleRoot(w, req)\n1724\t\n1725\t\tif w.Code != http.StatusOK {\n1726\t\t\tt.Fatalf(\"PUT failed with status %d\", w.Code)\n1727\t\t}\n1728\t\n1729\t\t// Get the ETag from the PUT response\n1730\t\tetag := strings.Trim(w.Header().Get(\"ETag\"), `\"`)\n1731\t\n1732\t\t// Test 1: If-Match with matching ETag - should succeed\n1733\t\treq = httptest.NewRequest(http.MethodGet, \"/test-bucket/conditional-test\", nil)\n1734\t\treq.Header.Set(\"If-Match\", fmt.Sprintf(`\"%s\"`, etag))\n1735\t\tw = httptest.NewRecorder()\n1736\t\th.HandleRoot(w, req)\n1737\t\n1738\t\tif w.Code != http.StatusOK {\n1739\t\t\tt.Errorf(\"If-Match with matching ETag: expected status 200, got %d\", w.Code)\n1740\t\t}\n1741\t\tif !bytes.Equal(w.Body.Bytes(), plaintext) {\n1742\t\t\tt.Error(\"If-Match with matching ETag: content mismatch\")\n1743\t\t}\n1744\t\n1745\t\t// Test 2: If-Match with non-matching ETag - should fail with 412\n1746\t\treq = httptest.NewRequest(http.MethodGet, \"/test-bucket/conditional-test\", nil)\n1747\t\treq.Header.Set(\"If-Match\", `\"wrong-etag\"`)\n1748\t\tw = httptest.NewRecorder()\n1749\t\th.HandleRoot(w, req)\n1750\t\n1751\t\tif w.Code != http.StatusPreconditionFailed {\n1752\t\t\tt.Errorf(\"If-Match with non-matching ETag: expected status 412, got %d\", w.Code)\n1753\t\t}\n1754\t\n1755\t\t// Test 3: If-Match with * (match any) - should succeed\n1756\t\treq = httptest.NewRequest(http.MethodGet, \"/test-bucket/conditional-test\", nil)\n1757\t\treq.Header.Set(\"If-Match\", \"*\")\n1758\t\tw = httptest.NewRecorder()\n1759\t\th.HandleRoot(w, req)\n1760\t\n1761\t\tif w.Code != http.StatusOK {\n1762\t\t\tt.Errorf(\"If-Match *: expected status 200, got %d\", w.Code)\n1763\t\t}\n1764\t\n1765\t\t// Test 4: If-None-Match with matching ETag - should return 304\n1766\t\treq = httptest.NewRequest(http.MethodGet, \"/test-bucket/conditional-test\", nil)\n1767\t\treq.Header.Set(\"If-None-Match\", fmt.Sprintf(`\"%s\"`, etag))\n1768\t\tw = httptest.NewRecorder()\n1769\t\th.HandleRoot(w, req)\n1770\t\n1771\t\tif w.Code != http.StatusNotModified {\n1772\t\t\tt.Errorf(\"If-None-Match with matching ETag: expected status 304, got %d\", w.Code)\n1773\t\t}\n1774\t\tif w.Body.Len() != 0 {\n1775\t\t\tt.Error(\"If-None-Match with matching ETag: expected empty body\")\n1776\t\t}\n1777\t\n1778\t\t// Test 5: If-None-Match with non-matching ETag - should succeed\n1779\t\treq = httptest.NewRequest(http.MethodGet, \"/test-bucket/conditional-test\", nil)\n1780\t\treq.Header.Set(\"If-None-Match\", `\"wrong-etag\"`)\n1781\t\tw = httptest.NewRecorder()\n1782\t\th.HandleRoot(w, req)\n1783\t\n1784\t\tif w.Code != http.StatusOK {\n1785\t\t\tt.Errorf(\"If-None-Match with non-matching ETag: expected status 200, got %d\", w.Code)\n1786\t\t}\n1787\t\n1788\t\t// Test 6: If-Modified-Since with future date - should return 304\n1789\t\tfutureTime := time.Now().Add(24 * time.Hour).UTC().Format(http.TimeFormat)\n1790\t\treq = httptest.NewRequest(http.MethodGet, \"/test-bucket/conditional-test\", nil)\n1791\t\treq.Header.Set(\"If-Modified-Since\", futureTime)\n1792\t\tw = httptest.NewRecorder()\n1793\t\th.HandleRoot(w, req)\n1794\t\n1795\t\tif w.Code != http.StatusNotModified {\n1796\t\t\tt.Errorf(\"If-Modified-Since with future date: expected status 304, got %d\", w.Code)\n1797\t\t}\n1798\t\n1799\t\t// Test 7: If-Modified-Since with past date - should succeed\n1800\t\tpastTime := time.Now().Add(-24 * time.Hour).UTC().Format(http.TimeFormat)\n1801\t\treq = httptest.NewRequest(http.MethodGet, \"/test-bucket/conditional-test\", nil)\n1802\t\treq.Header.Set(\"If-Modified-Since\", pastTime)\n1803\t\tw = httptest.NewRecorder()\n1804\t\th.HandleRoot(w, req)\n1805\t\n1806\t\tif w.Code != http.StatusOK {\n1807\t\t\tt.Errorf(\"If-Modified-Since with past date: expected status 200, got %d\", w.Code)\n1808\t\t}\n1809\t\n1810\t\t// Test 8: If-Unmodified-Since with future date - should succeed\n1811\t\treq = httptest.NewRequest(http.MethodGet, \"/test-bucket/conditional-test\", nil)\n1812\t\treq.Header.Set(\"If-Unmodified-Since\", futureTime)\n1813\t\tw = httptest.NewRecorder()\n1814\t\th.HandleRoot(w, req)\n1815\t\n1816\t\tif w.Code != http.StatusOK {\n1817\t\t\tt.Errorf(\"If-Unmodified-Since with future date: expected status 200, got %d\", w.Code)\n1818\t\t}\n1819\t\n1820\t\t// Test 9: If-Unmodified-Since with past date - Produces visualizations that read as one system — elegant, accessible, consistent in light and dark — using a brand-neutral placeholder palette you swap for your own. Teaches a design-system-agnostic method: a form heuristic, a color formula with a runnable validator, mark specs, and interaction rules. A validated default palette is documented in `references/palette.md` — swap that file's values for your brand's. Triggers on: \\\"chart\\\", \\\"graph\\\", \\\"plot\\\", \\\"data viz\\\", \\\"visualization\\\", \\\"dashboard\\\", \\\"analytics\\\", \\\"visualize data\\\", \\\"categorical colors\\\", \\\"sequential / diverging palette\\\", \\\"stat tile\\\", \\\"sparkline\\\", \\\"heatmap\\\", \\\"legend\\\", \\\"axis\\\", \\\"tooltip\\\", \\\"chart colors\\\", \\\"color by series\\\".\\n- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors (\\\"from now on when X\\\", \\\"each time X\\\", \\\"whenever X\\\", \\\"before/after X\\\") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions (\\\"allow X\\\", \\\"add permission\\\", \\\"move permission to\\\"), env vars (\\\"set X=Y\\\"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: \\\"allow npm commands\\\", \\\"add bq permission to global settings\\\", \\\"move permission to user settings\\\", \\\"set DEBUG=true\\\", \\\"when claude stops show X\\\". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.\\n- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: \\\"rebind ctrl+s\\\", \\\"add a chord shortcut\\\", \\\"change the submit key\\\", \\\"customize keybindings\\\".\\n- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by exercising it end-to-end and observing behavior — drive the affected flow, not just tests or typecheck. Run before committing nontrivial changes; bootstraps this repo's project verify skill if none exists yet. Don't invoke it on a diff that only touches tests, docs, or other code with no runtime surface to drive (a change to product source always has one) — there's nothing to observe.\\n- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.\\n- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.\\n- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.\\n- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo, defaults to 10m) - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. \\\"check the deploy every 5 minutes\\\", \\\"keep running /babysit-prs\\\"). modified\\\")\\n756\\t\\t}\\n757\\t}\\n758\\t\\n759\\tfunc TestETagConsistency(t *testing.T) {\\n760\\t\\t// Test that ETag is consistent across uploads of same content\\n761\\t\\tcfg, mb, cache, twice\\n767\\t\\treq1 := httptest.NewRequest(http.MethodPut, \\\"/test-bucket/etag-test-1\\\", bytes.NewReader(content))\\n768\\t\\treq1.Header.Set(\\\"Content-Type\\\", \\\"text/plain\\\")\\n769\\t\\tw1 := httptest.NewRecorder()\\n770\\t\\th.HandleRoot(w1, req1)\\n771\\t\\tetag1 := w1.Header().Get(\\\"ETag\\\")\\n772\\t\\n773\\t\\treq2 := httptest.NewRequest(http.MethodPut, \\\"/test-bucket/etag-test-2\\\", bytes.NewReader(content))\\n774\\t\\treq2.Header.Set(\\\"Content-Type\\\", \\\"text/plain\\\")\\n775\\t\\tw2 := httptest.NewRecorder()\\n776\\t\\th.HandleRoot(w2, req2)\\n777\\t\\tetag2 := w2.Header().Get(\\\"ETag\\\")\\n778\\t\\n779\\t\\tif etag1 != etag2 {\\n780\\t\\t\\tt.Errorf(\\\"ETags for same content differ: %s vs %s\\\", etag1, etag2)\\n781\\t\\t}\\n782\\t\\n783\\t\\t// Upload different content\\n784\\t\\tdifferentContent := []byte(\\\"Different content\\\")\\n785\\t\\treq3 := httptest.NewRequest(http.MethodPut, \\\"/test-bucket/etag-test-3\\\", bytes.NewReader(differentContent))\\n786\\t\\treq3.Header.Set(\\\"Content-Type\\\", \\\"text/plain\\\")\\n787\\t\\tw3 := httptest.NewRecorder()\\n788\\t\\th.HandleRoot(w3, req3)\\n789\\t\\tetag3 := w3.Header().Get(\\\"ETag\\\")\\n790\\t\\n791\\t\\tif etag1 == etag3 {\\n792\\t\\t\\tt.Error(\\\"ETags for different content are the same\\\")\\n793\\t\\t}\\n794\\t}\\n795\\t\\n796\\t// TestHMACVerification ensures HMAC verification catches tampering\\n797\\t// With streaming decryption, the HTTP status is written before all blocks are verified.\\n798\\t// Tampering is detected during streaming, which will cause the body to be incomplete.\\n799\\t// The httptest.ResponseRecorder doesn't capture streaming errors, so we verify\\n800\\t// that the body content is incomplete or corrupted rather than checking status code.\\n801\\tfunc TestHMACVerification(t *testing.T) {\\n802\\t\\tcfg, mb, cache, footerCache, mek := testSetup(t)\\n803\\t\\th := handlers.New(cfg, mb, cache, footerCache, mek, nil)\\n804\\t\\n805\\t\\tplaintext := []byte(\\\"Content to verify\\\")\\n806\\t\\n807\\t\\treq := httptest.NewRequest(http.MethodPut, \\\"/test-bucket/verify-test\\\", bytes.NewReader(plaintext))\\n808\\t\\treq.Header.Set(\\\"Content-Type\\\", \\\"text/plain\\\")\\n809\\t\\tw := httptest.NewRecorder()\\n810\\t\\th.HandleRoot(w, req)\\n811\\t\\n812\\t\\t// Tamper with stored data\\n813\\t\\tmb.mu.Lock()\\n814\\t\\tstored := mb.objects[\\\"test-bucket/verify-test\\\"]\\n815\\t\\t// Modify a byte in the encrypted data section (after header)\\n816\\t\\tif len(stored) > 100 {\\n817\\t\\t\\tstored[80] ^= 0xFF\\n818\\t\\t}\\n819\\t\\tmb.mu.Unlock()\\n820\\t\\n821\\t\\t// GET should fail integrity check\\n822\\t\\t// With streaming, the status is written before HMAC verification completes.\\n823\\t\\t// The verification error will cause incomplete/corrupted body content.\\n824\\t\\treq = httptest.NewRequest(http.MethodGet, \\\"/test-bucket/verify-test\\\", nil)\\n825\\t\\tw = httptest.NewRecorder()\\n826\\t\\th.HandleRoot(w, req)\\n827\\t\\n828\\t\\t// Verify the response is not the original plaintext (tampering detected)\\n829\\t\\t// Either the body is empty/incomplete, or it's corrupted\\n830\\t\\tif bytes.Equal(w.Body.Bytes(), plaintext) {\\n831\\t\\t\\tt.Error(\\\"expected corrupted/incomplete data for tampered object, got original plaintext\\\")\\n832\\t\\t}\\n833\\t\\n834\\t\\t// For range requests, we still get 500 because HMAC is verified before streaming\\n835\\t\\t// But for full downloads with streaming, we may get 200 with incomplete body\\n836\\t\\t// This is expected behavior - streaming provides lower latency at the cost of\\n837\\t\\t// potentially incomplete responses on integrity failure\\n838\\t}\\n839\\t\\n840\\t// TestRangeSuffixRequest tests suffix range requests (e.g., \\\"bytes=-100\\\")\\n841\\tfunc TestRangeSuffixRequest(t *testing.T) {\\n842\\t\\tcfg, mb, cache, footerCache, mek := testSetup(t)\\n843\\t\\th := handlers.New(cfg, mb, cache, footerCache, mek, nil)\\n844\\t\\n845\\t\\tplaintext := make([]byte, 10000)\\n846\\t\\tfor i := range plaintext {\\n847\\t\\t\\tplaintext[i] = byte(i % 256)\\n848\\t\\t}\\n849\\t\\n850\\t\\treq := httptest.NewRequest(http.MethodPut, \\\"/test-bucket/suffix-test\\\", bytes.NewReader(plaintext))\\n851\\t\\treq.Header.Set(\\\"Content-Type\\\", \\\"application/octet-stream\\\")\\n852\\t\\tw := httptest.NewRecorder()\\n853\\t\\th.HandleRoot(w, req)\\n854\\t\\n855\\t\\t// Request last 100 bytes\\n856\\t\\treq = httptest.NewRequest(http.MethodGet, \\\"/test-bucket/suffix-test\\\", nil)\\n857\\t\\treq.Header.Set(\\\"Range\\\", \\\"bytes=-100\\\")\\n858\\t\\tw = httptest.NewRecorder()\\n859\\t\\n860\\t\\th.HandleRoot(w, req)\\n861\\t\\n862\\t\\tif w.Code != http.StatusPartialContent {\\n863\\t\\t\\tt.Errorf(\\\"expected This exercises the io.Pipe based streaming path.\\n874\\tfunc TestStreamingDecryption(t *testing.T) {\\n875\\t\\tcfg, mb, cache, footerCache, mek := testSetup(t)\\n876\\t\\th := handlers.New(cfg, mb, cache, footerCache, mek, nil)\\n877\\t\\n878\\t\\t// Create a file spanning multiple blocks (64KB each)\\n879\\t\\t// Using 3 blocks worth of data\\n880\\t\\tblockSize := cfg.BlockSize\\n881\\t\\tplaintext := make([]byte, blockSize*3-1000) // Not aligned to block boundary\\n882\\t\\tfor i := range plaintext {\\n883\\t\\t\\tplaintext[i] = byte(i % 256)\\n884\\t\\t}\\n885\\t\\n886\\t\\t// Upload\\n887\\t\\treq := httptest.NewRequest(http.MethodPut, \\\"/test-bucket/streaming-test\\\", req)\\n891\\t\\n892\\t\\tif w.Code != http.StatusOK {\\n893\\t\\t\\tt.Fatalf(\\\"PUT failed: status %d\\\", w.Code)\\n894\\t\\t}\\n895\\t\\n896\\t\\t// Download with full streaming (no Range w.Code != http.StatusOK {\\n902\\t\\t\\tt.Errorf(\\\"GET failed: status %d\\\", w.Code)\\n903\\t\\t}\\n904\\t\\n905\\t\\t// Verify streaming header is present\\n906\\t\\tif for empty bucket\\\")\\n1655\\t\\t}\\n1656\\t\\n1657\\t\\t// Empty bucket should have no objects\\n1658\\t\\tif len(result.Contents) != 0 {\\n1659\\t\\t\\tt.Errorf(\\\"expected 0 objects in empty bucket, got %d\\\", len(result.Contents))\\n1660\\t\\t}\\n1661\\t\\n1662\\t\\t// Empty bucket should have no common prefixes\\n1663\\t\\tif len(result.CommonPrefixes) != 0 {\\n1664\\t\\t\\tt.Errorf(\\\"expected 0 common prefixes in empty bucket, got %d\\\", len(result.CommonPrefixes))\\n1665\\t\\t}\\n1666\\t\\n1667\\t\\t// Verify response is well-formed XML\\n1668\\t\\tif !strings.HasPrefix(w.Body.String(), `<?xml version=\\\"1.0\\\" encoding=\\\"UTF-8\\\"?>`) {\\n1669\\t\\t\\tt.Error(\\\"expected XML declaration in response\\\")\\n1670\\t\\t}\\n1671\\t\\n1672\\t\\t// Test with prefix filter on empty bucket\\n1673\\t\\treq = httptest.NewRequest(http.MethodGet, \\\"/test-bucket?list-type=2&prefix=data/\\\", nil)\\n1674\\t\\tw = httptest.NewRecorder()\\n1675\\t\\n1676\\t\\th.HandleRoot(w, req)\\n1677\\t\\n1678\\t\\tif w.Code != http.StatusOK {\\n1679\\t\\t\\tt.Errorf(\\\"expected status 200 for empty bucket with prefix, got %d\\\", w.Code)\\n1680\\t\\t}\\n1681\\t\\n1682\\t\\t// Parse and verify empty response with prefix\\n1683\\t\\tvar prefixResult struct {\\n1684\\t\\t\\tPrefix   string `xml:\\\"Prefix\\\"`\\n1685\\t\\t\\tContents []struct {\\n1686\\t\\t\\t\\tKey string `xml:\\\"Key\\\"`\\n1687\\t\\t\\t} `xml:\\\"Contents\\\"`\\n1688\\t\\t}\\n1689\\t\\n1690\\t\\tif err := xml.Unmarshal(w.Body.Bytes(), &prefixResult); err != nil {\\n1691\\t\\t\\tt.Fatalf(\\\"failed to parse XML with prefix: %v\\\", err)\\n1692\\t\\t}\\n1693\\t\\n1694\\t\\tif prefixResult.Prefix != \\\"data/\\\" {\\n1695\\t\\t\\tt.Errorf(\\\"expected prefix 'data/', got '%s'\\\", prefixResult.Prefix)\\n1696\\t\\t}\\n1697\\t\\n1698\\t\\tif len(prefixResult.Contents) != 0 {\\n1699\\t\\t\\tt.Errorf(\\\"expected 0 objects with prefix filter on empty bucket, got %d\\\", len(prefixResult.Contents))\\n1700\\t\\t}\\n1701\\t\\n1702\\t\\t// Test with delimiter on empty bucket\\n1703\\t\\treq = httptest.NewRequest(http.MethodGet, \\\"/test-bucket?list-type=2&delimiter=/\\\", nil)\\n1704\\t\\tw = httptest.NewRecorder()\\n1705\\t\\n1706\\t\\th.HandleRoot(w, req)\\n1707\\t\\n1708\\t\\tif w.Code != http.StatusOK {\\n1709\\t\\t\\tt.Errorf(\\\"expected status 200 for empty bucket with delimiter, got %d\\\", w.Code)\\n1710\\t\\t}\\n1711\\t}\\n1712\\t\\n1713\\t// TestConditionalRequests tests If-Match, If-None-Match, If-Modified-Since, If-Unmodified-Since\\n1714\\tfunc TestConditionalRequests(t *testing.T) {\\n1715\\t\\tcfg, mb, cache, footerCache, mek := testSetup(t)\\n1716\\t\\th := handlers.New(cfg, mb, cache, footerCache, mek, nil)\\n1717\\t\\n1718\\t\\t// Create and upload an object\\n1719\\t\\tplaintext := []byte(\\\"Hello, ARMOR! This is a test file for conditional requests.\\\")\\n1720\\t\\treq := httptest.NewRequest(http.MethodPut, \\\"/test-bucket/conditional-test\\\", bytes.NewReader(plaintext))\\n1721\\t\\treq.Header.Set(\\\"Content-Type\\\", \\\"text/plain\\\")\\n1722\\t\\tw := httptest.NewRecorder()\\n1723\\t\\th.HandleRoot(w, req)\\n1724\\t\\n1725\\t\\tif w.Code != http.StatusOK {\\n1726\\t\\t\\tt.Fatalf(\\\"PUT failed with status %d\\\", w.Code)\\n1727\\t\\t}\\n1728\\t\\n1729\\t\\t// Get the ETag from the PUT response\\n1730\\t\\tetag := strings.Trim(w.Header().Get(\\\"ETag\\\"), `\\\"`)\\n1731\\t\\n1732\\t\\t// Test 1: If-Match with matching ETag - should succeed\\n1733\\t\\treq = httptest.NewRequest(http.MethodGet, \\\"/test-bucket/conditional-test\\\", nil)\\n1734\\t\\treq.Header.Set(\\\"If-Match\\\", fmt.Sprintf(`\\\"%s\\\"`, etag))\\n1735\\t\\tw = httptest.NewRecorder()\\n1736\\t\\th.HandleRoot(w, req)\\n1737\\t\\n1738\\t\\tif w.Code != http.StatusOK {\\n1739\\t\\t\\tt.Errorf(\\\"If-Match with matching ETag: expected status 200, got %d\\\", w.Code)\\n1740\\t\\t}\\n1741\\t\\tif !bytes.Equal(w.Body.Bytes(), plaintext) {\\n1742\\t\\t\\tt.Error(\\\"If-Match with matching ETag: content mismatch\\\")\\n1743\\t\\t}\\n1744\\t\\n1745\\t\\t// Test 2: If-Match with non-matching ETag - should fail with 412\\n1746\\t\\treq = httptest.NewRequest(http.MethodGet, \\\"/test-bucket/conditional-test\\\", nil)\\n1747\\t\\treq.Header.Set(\\\"If-Match\\\", `\\\"wrong-etag\\\"`)\\n1748\\t\\tw = httptest.NewRecorder()\\n1749\\t\\th.HandleRoot(w, req)\\n1750\\t\\n1751\\t\\tif w.Code != http.StatusPreconditionFailed {\\n1752\\t\\t\\tt.Errorf(\\\"If-Match with non-matching ETag: expected status 412, got %d\\\", w.Code)\\n1753\\t\\t}\\n1754\\t\\n1755\\t\\t// Test 3: If-Match with * (match any) - should succeed\\n1756\\t\\treq = httptest.NewRequest(http.MethodGet, \\\"/test-bucket/conditional-test\\\", nil)\\n1757\\t\\treq.Header.Set(\\\"If-Match\\\", \\\"*\\\")\\n1758\\t\\tw = httptest.NewRecorder()\\n1759\\t\\th.HandleRoot(w, req)\\n1760\\t\\n1761\\t\\tif w.Code != http.StatusOK {\\n1762\\t\\t\\tt.Errorf(\\\"If-Match *: expected status 200, got %d\\\", w.Code)\\n1763\\t\\t}\\n1764\\t\\n1765\\t\\t// Test 4: If-None-Match with matching ETag - should return 304\\n1766\\t\\treq = httptest.NewRequest(http.MethodGet, \\\"/test-bucket/conditional-test\\\", nil)\\n1767\\t\\treq.Header.Set(\\\"If-None-Match\\\", fmt.Sprintf(`\\\"%s\\\"`, etag))\\n1768\\t\\tw = httptest.NewRecorder()\\n1769\\t\\th.HandleRoot(w, req)\\n1770\\t\\n1771\\t\\tif w.Code != http.StatusNotModified {\\n1772\\t\\t\\tt.Errorf(\\\"If-None-Match with matching ETag: expected status 304, got %d\\\", w.Code)\\n1773\\t\\t}\\n1774\\t\\tif w.Body.Len() != 0 {\\n1775\\t\\t\\tt.Error(\\\"If-None-Match with matching ETag: expected empty body\\\")\\n1776\\t\\t}\\n1777\\t\\n1778\\t\\t// Test 5: If-None-Match with non-matching ETag - should succeed\\n1779\\t\\treq = httptest.NewRequest(http.MethodGet, \\\"/test-bucket/conditional-test\\\", nil)\\n1780\\t\\treq.Header.Set(\\\"If-None-Match\\\", `\\\"wrong-etag\\\"`)\\n1781\\t\\tw = httptest.NewRecorder()\\n1782\\t\\th.HandleRoot(w, req)\\n1783\\t\\n1784\\t\\tif w.Code != http.StatusOK {\\n1785\\t\\t\\tt.Errorf(\\\"If-None-Match with non-matching ETag: expected status 200, got %d\\\", w.Code)\\n1786\\t\\t}\\n1787\\t\\n1788\\t\\t// Test 6: If-Modified-Since with future date - should return 304\\n1789\\t\\tfutureTime := time.Now().Add(24 * time.Hour).UTC().Format(http.TimeFormat)\\n1790\\t\\treq = httptest.NewRequest(http.MethodGet, \\\"/test-bucket/conditional-test\\\", nil)\\n1791\\t\\treq.Header.Set(\\\"If-Modified-Since\\\", futureTime)\\n1792\\t\\tw = httptest.NewRecorder()\\n1793\\t\\th.HandleRoot(w, req)\\n1794\\t\\n1795\\t\\tif w.Code != http.StatusNotModified {\\n1796\\t\\t\\tt.Errorf(\\\"If-Modified-Since with future date: expected status 304, got %d\\\", w.Code)\\n1797\\t\\t}\\n1798\\t\\n1799\\t\\t// Test 7: If-Modified-Since with past date - should succeed\\n1800\\t\\tpastTime := time.Now().Add(-24 * time.Hour).UTC().Format(http.TimeFormat)\\n1801\\t\\treq = httptest.NewRequest(http.MethodGet, \\\"/test-bucket/conditional-test\\\", nil)\\n1802\\t\\treq.Header.Set(\\\"If-Modified-Since\\\", pastTime)\\n1803\\t\\tw = httptest.NewRecorder()\\n1804\\t\\th.HandleRoot(w, req)\\n1805\\t\\n1806\\t\\tif w.Code != http.StatusOK {\\n1807\\t\\t\\tt.Errorf(\\\"If-Modified-Since with past date: expected status 200, got %d\\\", w.Code)\\n1808\\t\\t}\\n1809\\t\\n1810\\t\\t// Test 8: If-Unmodified-Since with future date - should succeed\\n1811\\t\\treq = httptest.NewRequest(http.MethodGet, \\\"/test-bucket/conditional-test\\\", nil)\\n1812\\t\\treq.Header.Set(\\\"If-Unmodified-Since\\\", futureTime)\\n1813\\t\\tw = httptest.NewRecorder()\\n1814\\t\\th.HandleRoot(w, req)\\n1815\\t\\n1816\\t\\tif w.Code != http.StatusOK {\\n1817\\t\\t\\tt.Errorf(\\\"If-Unmodified-Since with future date: expected status 200, got %d\\\", w.Code)\\n1818\\t\\t}\\n1819\\t\\n1820\\t\\t// Test 9: If-Unmodified-Since with past date - in the mock backend\\n739\\t\\tplainData := []byte(\\\"Plain unencrypted data\\\")\\n740\\t\\tmb.Put(context.Background(), \\\"test-bucket\\\", \\\"plain-file\\\", bytes.NewReader(plainData), {\\n751\\t\\t\\tt.Errorf(\\\"expected status 200, got %d\\\", w.Code)\\n752\\t\\t}\\n753\\t\\n754\\t\\tif !bytes.Equal(w.Body.Bytes(), plainData) {\\n755\\t\\t\\tt.Error(\\\"plain data was modified\\\")\\n756\\t\\t}\\n757\\t}\\n758\\t\\n759\\tfunc TestETagConsistency(t *testing.T) {\\n760\\t\\t// Test that ETag is consistent across uploads of same content\\n761\\t\\tcfg, mb, cache, footerCache, mek := testSetup(t)\\n762\\t\\th := handlers.New(cfg, mb, cache, footerCache, mek, nil)\\n763\\t\\n764\\t\\tcontent := []byte(\\\"Same content\\\")\\n765\\t\\n766\\t\\t// Upload twice\\n767\\t\\treq1 := httptest.NewRequest(http.MethodPut, \\\"/test-bucket/etag-test-1\\\", bytes.NewReader(content))\\n768\\t\\treq1.Header.Set(\\\"Content-Type\\\", \\\"text/plain\\\")\\n769\\t\\tw1 := httptest.NewRecorder()\\n770\\t\\th.HandleRoot(w1, req1)\\n771\\t\\tetag1 := w1.Header().Get(\\\"ETag\\\")\\n772\\t\\n773\\t\\treq2 := httptest.NewRequest(http.MethodPut, \\\"/test-bucket/etag-test-2\\\", bytes.NewReader(content))\\n774\\t\\treq2.Header.Set(\\\"Content-Type\\\", \\\"text/plain\\\")\\n775\\t\\tw2 := httptest.NewRecorder()\\n776\\t\\th.HandleRoot(w2, req2)\\n777\\t\\tetag2 := w2.Header().Get(\\\"ETag\\\")\\n778\\t\\n779\\t\\tif etag1 != etag2 {\\n780\\t\\t\\tt.Errorf(\\\"ETags for same content differ: %s vs %s\\\", etag1, etag2)\\n781\\t\\t}\\n782\\t\\n783\\t\\t// Upload different content\\n784\\t\\tdifferentContent := []byte(\\\"Different content\\\")\\n785\\t\\treq3 := httptest.NewRequest(http.MethodPut, \\\"/test-bucket/etag-test-3\\\", bytes.NewReader(differentContent))\\n786\\t\\treq3.Header.Set(\\\"Content-Type\\\", \\\"text/plain\\\")\\n787\\t\\tw3 := httptest.NewRecorder()\\n788\\t\\th.HandleRoot(w3, req3)\\n789\\t\\tetag3 := w3.Header().Get(\\\"ETag\\\")\\n790\\t\\n791\\t\\tif etag1 == etag3 {\\n792\\t\\t\\tt.Error(\\\"ETags for different content are the same\\\")\\n793\\t\\t}\\n794\\t}\\n795\\t\\n796\\t// TestHMACVerification ensures HMAC verification catches tampering\\n797\\t// With streaming decryption, the HTTP status is written before all blocks are verified.\\n798\\t// Tampering is detected during streaming, which will cause the body to be incomplete.\\n799\\t// The httptest.ResponseRecorder doesn't capture streaming errors, so we verify\\n800\\t// that the body content is incomplete or corrupted rather than checking status code.\\n801\\tfunc TestHMACVerification(t *testing.T) {\\n802\\t\\tcfg, mb, cache, footerCache, mek := testSetup(t)\\n803\\t\\th := handlers.New(cfg, mb, cache, footerCache, mek, nil)\\n804\\t\\n805\\t\\tplaintext := []byte(\\\"Content to verify\\\")\\n806\\t\\n807\\t\\treq := httptest.NewRequest(http.MethodPut, \\\"/test-bucket/verify-test\\\", bytes.NewReader(plaintext))\\n808\\t\\treq.Header.Set(\\\"Content-Type\\\", \\\"text/plain\\\")\\n809\\t\\tw := httptest.NewRecorder()\\n810\\t\\th.HandleRoot(w, req)\\n811\\t\\n812\\t\\t// Tamper with stored data\\n813\\t\\tmb.mu.Lock()\\n814\\t\\tstored := mb.objects[\\\"test-bucket/verify-test\\\"]\\n815\\t\\t// Modify a byte in the encrypted data section (after header)\\n816\\t\\tif len(stored) > 100 {\\n817\\t\\t\\tstored[80] ^= 0xFF\\n818\\t\\t}\\n819\\t\\tmb.mu.Unlock()\\n820\\t\\n821\\t\\t// GET should fail integrity check\\n822\\t\\t// With streaming, the status is written before HMAC verification completes.\\n823\\t\\t// The verification error will cause incomplete/corrupted body content.\\n824\\t\\treq = httptest.NewRequest(http.MethodGet, \\\"/test-bucket/verify-test\\\", nil)\\n825\\t\\tw = httptest.NewRecorder()\\n826\\t\\th.HandleRoot(w, req)\\n827\\t\\n828\\t\\t// Verify the response is not the original plaintext (tampering detected)\\n829\\t\\t// Either the body is empty/incomplete, or it's corrupted\\n830\\t\\tif bytes.Equal(w.Body.Bytes(), plaintext) {\\n831\\t\\t\\tt.Error(\\\"expected corrupted/incomplete data for tampered object, got original plaintext\\\")\\n832\\t\\t}\\n833\\t\\n834\\t\\t// For range requests, we still get 500 because HMAC is verified before streaming\\n835\\t\\t// But for full downloads with streaming, we may get 200 with incomplete body\\n836\\t\\t// This is expected behavior - streaming provides lower latency at the cost of\\n837\\t\\t// potentially incomplete responses on integrity failure\\n838\\t}\\n839\\t\\n840\\t// TestRangeSuffixRequest tests suffix range requests (e.g., \\\"bytes=-100\\\")\\n841\\tfunc TestRangeSuffixRequest(t *testing.T) {\\n842\\t\\tcfg, mb, cache, footerCache, mek := testSetup(t)\\n843\\t\\th := handlers.New(cfg, mb, cache, footerCache, mek, nil)\\n844\\t\\n845\\t\\tplaintext := make([]byte, 10000)\\n846\\t\\tfor i := range plaintext {\\n847\\t\\t\\tplaintext[i] = byte(i % 256)\\n848\\t\\t}\\n849\\t\\n850\\t\\treq := httptest.NewRequest(http.MethodPut, \\\"/test-bucket/suffix-test\\\", bytes.NewReader(plaintext))\\n851\\t\\treq.Header.Set(\\\"Content-Type\\\", \\\"application/octet-stream\\\")\\n852\\t\\tw := httptest.NewRecorder()\\n853\\t\\th.HandleRoot(w, req)\\n854\\t\\n855\\t\\t// Request last 100 bytes\\n856\\t\\treq = httptest.NewRequest(http.MethodGet, \\\"/test-bucket/suffix-test\\\", nil)\\n857\\t\\treq.Header.Set(\\\"Range\\\",
